ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Animal Geneticists study the genetic makeup of animals to improve their breeding and productivity. They work with farmers, breeders, and researchers to develop new animal breeds and improve existing ones.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Animal Geneticists use advanced genetic techniques to analyze the DNA of animals and identify genes that influence desirable traits such as milk production, meat quality, and disease resistance. They work with a range of animals, including livestock, poultry, and fish, and use their knowledge to develop new breeding programs and improve existing ones.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: M.Sc. in Animal Genetics or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in animal science and genetics can pursue a degree in animal science or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree in a life science or related field can pursue a postgraduate degree in animal genetics or a related field.

future_builder.description: Animal Geneticists with experience in the field can advance to senior roles such as team lead or research scientist.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience as an Animal Geneticist

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into animal genetics by acquiring relevant skills and experience.
survival_skills_needed: Programming skills in R or Python
portfolio_proof_required: Project on animal breeding and genetics
